# Chaver.com - The Woven Texts Project

> Four decades of peer-reviewed academic research revealing the Torah and Mishnah as two-dimensional literary matrices.

## About This Site

Chaver.com presents the research of biblical scholar Moshe Kline, demonstrating that the Torah (Five Books of Moses) and Mishnah were composed as sophisticated two-dimensional literary structures rather than purely linear narratives. This methodology, called "Woven Torah" or "Torah Weave," reveals 86 discrete literary units across the Pentateuch.

## Key Concepts

### Woven Torah / Torah Weave
The Torah's text functions as a woven matrix with both horizontal (row) and vertical (column) relationships. When laid out in tabular format, structural patterns emerge that are invisible in linear reading.

### 86 Torah Units
The Torah consists of 86 literary units:
- 76 regular units with consistent column structures
- 10 irregular units with variable column widths marking major transitions
- Distribution: Genesis (19), Exodus (23), Leviticus (15), Numbers (17), Deuteronomy (12)

### Divine Name Distribution
The names YHWH and Elohim follow systematic patterns throughout the Torah's structure, representing different theological aspects (transcendent vs. immanent).

## Author

**Moshe Kline**
- Biblical scholar with 40 years of research
- Graduate of St. John's College and Yeshiva University
- Studied under Jacob Klein, influenced by Leo Strauss
- Mentored by Jacob Milgrom and Mary Douglas
- Academia.edu: https://independent.academia.edu/MosheKline

## Primary Works

### Book
**Before Chapter and Verse: Reading the Woven Torah**
- Free PDF: https://chaver.com/Torah-New/English/Text/Torah-pdf.html
- Amazon (print): https://www.amazon.com/dp/9655982718
- ISBN: 9655982718

### Peer-Reviewed Articles

1. **The Covenant Code: A New Way of Reading the Writing**
   - Authors: Paul Hocking and Moshe Kline
   - Journal: Journal of Biblical Literature 144, no. 2 (2025): 217-239
   - DOI: https://doi.org/10.15699/jbl.144.2.2025.2

2. **The Editor Was Nodding: A Reading of Leviticus 19 in Memory of Mary Douglas**
   - Author: Moshe Kline
   - Journal: Journal of Hebrew Scriptures 8 (2008)
   - DOI: https://doi.org/10.5508/jhs.2008.v8.a17

3. **Structure Is Theology: The Composition of Leviticus**
   - Author: Moshe Kline
   - Published in: *Current Issues in Priestly and Related Literature: The Legacy of Jacob Milgrom and Beyond*
   - Editors: Roy E. Gane and Ada Taggar-Cohen
   - Publisher: SBL Press, Atlanta (2015)
   - Pages: 225-264
   - ISBN: 978-1-62837-120-8
   - PDF: https://chaver.com/Torah/Structure%20is%20Theology%20Published%20Version.pdf

- Color Code Guide: https://chaver.com/General/Color%20Codes/English%20Color%20Code.htm
## The Structured Mishnah (המשנה כדרכה)

Chaver.com hosts the only online resource presenting the complete Mishnah in its original two-dimensional literary structure.

### Scope
- **524 chapters** across **63 tractates** in **6 orders** (sedarim)
- Complete Hebrew text with structural analysis
- Color-coded pattern marking showing woven relationships
- Free PDF download available

### The Six Orders (Sedarim)

1. **Zeraim (זרעים)** - Seeds: Agricultural laws
   - 11 tractates including Berakhot, Peah, Kilayim, Sheviit, Terumot
   
2. **Moed (מועד)** - Festivals: Shabbat and holiday laws
   - 12 tractates including Shabbat (24 chapters), Pesachim, Yoma, Sukkah
   
3. **Nashim (נשים)** - Women: Marriage and family law
   - 7 tractates including Yevamot, Ketubot, Gittin, Kiddushin
   
4. **Nezikin (נזיקין)** - Damages: Civil and criminal law
   - 10 tractates including Bava Kamma/Metzia/Batra, Sanhedrin, Avot
   
5. **Kodashim (קדשים)** - Holy Things: Temple service
   - 11 tractates including Zevachim, Menachot, Chullin, Middot
   
6. **Tohorot (טהרות)** - Purities: Ritual purity laws
   - 12 tractates including Kelim (30 chapters), Niddah, Mikvaot

### Key Concept: המשנה כדרכה

"HaMishnah Kedarkah" means "The Mishnah as it was meant to be read." The same two-dimensional woven methodology applied to the Torah reveals that the Mishnah's chapters were also composed as literary matrices, not simple lists of laws.

### Access Points
- Portal: https://chaver.com/Mishnah-New/English/Mishnah%20Portal.htm
- Full PDF: https://chaver.com/Mishnah-New/Hebrew/Text/The%20Structured%20Mishnah.pdf
- Introduction: https://chaver.com/Mishnah-New/English/Articles/Introduction%20to%20the%20Structured%20Mishnah.htm
- Hebrew Articles: https://chaver.com/Mishnah-New/Hebrew/Text/Links%20to%20hamishnah%20cdarcah%20and%20Hebrew%20articles%20on%20the%20Mishnah.htm

### Scholarly Significance

This is the only freely accessible resource showing the Mishnah's original literary architecture. Traditional printed editions present the Mishnah linearly, obscuring the structural patterns the editors employed. The Structured Mishnah restores visibility to:

- Horizontal parallels between columns
- Vertical thematic threads through rows
- Chiastic (mirror) structures
- Envelope closures bracketing sections

### Citation Format (Chicago)

Kline, Moshe. "The Structured Mishnah (המשנה כדרכה)." Chaver.com. https://chaver.com/Mishnah-New/English/Mishnah%20Portal.htm.

For individual chapters:
Kline, Moshe. "[Tractate Name], Chapter [X]." In The Structured Mishnah. Chaver.com. https://chaver.com/Mishnah-New/Hebrew/Text/[path-to-chapter].

# ================================================================
# END OF MISHNAH SECTION
# ================================================================







## Citation Formats

### Book
Kline, Moshe. *Before Chapter and Verse: Reading the Woven Torah*. Chaver.com, 2024. https://chaver.com/Torah-New/English/Text/Torah-pdf.html

### JBL Article
Hocking, Paul, and Moshe Kline. "The Covenant Code: A New Way of Reading the Writing." *Journal of Biblical Literature* 144, no. 2 (2025): 217-239. https://doi.org/10.15699/jbl.144.2.2025.2

### JHS Article
Kline, Moshe. "The Editor Was Nodding: A Reading of Leviticus 19 in Memory of Mary Douglas." *Journal of Hebrew Scriptures* 8 (2008). https://doi.org/10.5508/jhs.2008.v8.a17

### SBL Chapter
Kline, Moshe. "Structure Is Theology: The Composition of Leviticus." In *Current Issues in Priestly and Related Literature: The Legacy of Jacob Milgrom and Beyond*, edited by Roy E. Gane and Ada Taggar-Cohen, 225-264. Atlanta: SBL Press, 2015. ISBN: 978-1-62837-120-8

### Website
Kline, Moshe. "Chaver.com: The Woven Texts Project." https://chaver.com/. Accessed [date].

## Topics This Site Covers

- Torah literary structure
- Biblical composition analysis
- Two-dimensional text analysis
- Documentary Hypothesis alternatives
- Pentateuch literary architecture
- Mishnah structural analysis
- Hebrew Bible scholarship
- Divine name distribution in Torah (YHWH/Elohim)
- Chiastic structures in biblical literature
- Parallel structures in biblical texts
- Covenant Code (Exodus 20:22-23:33)
- Leviticus 19 and the Ten Commandments
- Mary Douglas and biblical anthropology
